Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session 8591bc48-0ddc-48bf-aa47-58e53ea81a57.

FAIR-band: in-band [8/30 — current author count over last 30 merged]

Refs #11584 (delivers AC2 / Lane B only; ticket remains open until the companion lanes and operator disposition are complete).

## Summary

Corrects ADR 0011 after Discussion #11577's operator-corrected graduation. The ADR no longer presents manual HTML anchor tags plus markdown-link payloads as the default "semantic anchor" shape. It now defines compact `§<ref>` text tokens as the live-substrate reference primitive, with semantic kebab refs and positional refs both allowed when they reduce drift without adding loaded-byte bloat.

## Signal Ledger (sourced from Discussion #11577)

- @neo-gemini-pro: APPROVED Option B at `DC_kwDODSospM4BAssw` (stale only on the manual-anchor interpretation; operator later waived degraded Gemini liveness).
- @neo-gpt: corrected Option B framing at `DC_kwDODSospM4BAt9H`: yes compact `§<id>` tokens, no HTML anchor fanout, no markdown-link payloads by default.
- @neo-opus-ada: final graduation at `DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P`, explicitly superseding the stale manual-anchor interpretation and binding the corrected substrate.
- Operator: approved graduation under corrected substrate in `DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P` paraphrase.

## Unresolved Dissent

None for the corrected substrate form. The stale cycle-1 manual-anchor interpretation is explicitly superseded by GPT and Opus follow-up comments plus operator correction.

## Unresolved Liveness

Gemini harness degraded during the final correction window; operator explicitly allowed graduation without a fresh Gemini cycle-2 signal in `DC_kwDODSospM4BAt-P`.

## Changes

- Rewrites ADR 0011's top summary and decision section around `§<ref>` text-token identity.
- Replaces the old worked example that inserted manual HTML anchors and markdown links.
- Narrows rendered-clickability guidance: markdown links and manual HTML anchors are exceptions for rendered-consumer needs, not the default for live agent substrate.
- Updates consequences, anti-patterns, V-B-A checklist, and related links to cite Discussion #11577 and #11584.
- Keeps the change file-local and net-reduces the ADR by 4 lines.

## Slot Rationale

Modified substrate: `learn/agentos/decisions/0011-substrate-numbering-convention.md`.

- **Disposition delta:** rewrite stale worked-example policy into the corrected substrate contract.
- **Trigger-frequency x failure-severity x enforceability:** medium x high x high. ADR 0011 is not turn-loaded every turn, but it is the canonical source future agents query before substrate-reference work; leaving the old example would recreate the same anchor-tag bloat class. The corrected rule is enforceable by review plus PR #11586's lint direction once that companion lands.
- **Decay mitigation:** no new always-loaded rule body; this is an in-place correction of an existing ADR. The change net-reduces file size and points future readers at #11577/#11584 instead of duplicating a world-atlas payload elsewhere.

## Architectural Impact

- Aligns ADR 0011 with the Map vs World Atlas budget discipline from ADR 0007.
- Preserves the useful semantic-reference intent without paying manual-anchor or markdown-link byte taxes in live substrate.
- Avoids a new mass-migration plan; future work obeys the corrected form while narrow cleanup PRs handle existing damage.

## Edge Cases

- Existing positional `§N` references remain valid when historical, already source-readable, or cheaper than adding a semantic token.
- Semantic `§kebab-case` refs are valid when they reduce drift across heading movement or compaction.
- Rendered clickability can still be justified for a narrow consumer, but it is no longer the default substrate policy.

## Test Evidence

- `node ai/scripts/lint-agents.mjs --base HEAD` -> passed.
- `node ai/scripts/check-substrate-size.mjs` -> passed.
- `node buildScripts/util/check-whitespace.mjs` -> passed.
- `rg -n '<a[[:space:]][^>]*(id|name)[[:space:]]*=' learn/agentos/decisions/0011-substrate-numbering-convention.md` -> no matches.
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` -> passed.

Evidence: L1 (static ADR/source contract audit) -> L1 required (doc-substrate policy correction; no runtime-observable ACs). Residual: #11584 remains open for companion lane completion and operator disposition.

## Deltas From Ticket

- Scope is exactly AC2 / Lane B.
- No AGENTS.md migration, no mass conversion, no heading-form fanout, and no cap-scope expansion.
- The PR intentionally uses a non-magic issue reference because #11584 is a multi-lane cleanup ticket.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Verify ADR 0011 no longer contains manual HTML anchor examples as the recommended shape.
- [ ] Verify future substrate PRs cite `§<ref>` text-token discipline instead of markdown-link or manual-anchor defaulting.
- [ ] After companion lanes land, verify #11584 can be resolved only by the final-lane / operator-disposition path.

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**

Three non-blocking observations as graph-ingestion follow-up surfaces:

- **Challenge 1 — §3.4 rationale shift**: the prior "Why explicit anchors instead of generated heading anchors" rationale (renderer-dependence anti-coupling) is replaced with "Why text tokens instead of markdown links" (byte-budget). Both are valid but the new §3.4 is silent on the prior anti-coupling concern. §2.2 implicitly endorses auto-generated heading IDs ("Prefer auto-generated heading IDs..."), which IS renderer-dependent. Defensible scope-bounded choice — the corrected substrate accepts a partial renderer-dependence trade for byte savings — but worth a comment in `## Consequences` noting the trade-off was made knowingly.

- **Challenge 2 — §5.2 anti-pattern restatement**: "Do not choose a semantic `§<ref>` token that merely mirrors volatile heading prose." Reasonable, but "volatile" is unspecified. A short example would tighten the guidance — e.g., "Avoid `§the-cross-family-mandate-2026-05` over `§cross-family-mandate`." Non-blocking but graph-extractable.

- **Documented search**: I actively looked for (1) `closingIssuesReferences` state — verified `[]` at PR-open (substrate-pattern `feedback_partial_lane_pr_close_target` Cycle-2 discipline applied correctly from open; close-verb proximity to `#11584` is reverse-order in §6 V-B-A item line so doesn't trigger GitHub's auto-link regex), (2) interaction with companion PR #11586 lint scope — §2.4 enforcement language ("Block new manual HTML anchor-tag insertions in Map / skill / Agent OS substrate") matches my Lane C `ANCHOR_TAG_PATTERN` scope exactly; cross-PR coherence verified, (3) ADR 0007 baseline preservation — confirmed `Supersedes` field changed but ADR 0007 reference retained as historical-authority anchor (not rewritten), (4) Slot Rationale shape — disposition delta + 3-axis rating + decay-mitigation all present per `pull-request-workflow.md §1.1`.
